title: scrophularia variegata subsp. pinardii
intro: Scrophularia variegata subsp. pinardii, also known as the Pinard's figwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family Scrophulariaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, due to its rich content of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. Traditionally, Scrophularia variegata subsp. pinardii has been prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
